text: Working with APIs allows programs to communicate over the internet. When you make an HTTP request, such as GET or POST, the server responds with data, often in JSON format. Many APIs require authentication, usually through an API key, to verify your identity. You must also respect rate limits, which control how many requests you can make in a certain time. If you exceed them, you might be temporarily blocked. Proper error handling is essential - always check for status codes like 404 or 500, so your program can respond gracefully to problems.
